summary: Rauch 说，设计正在变成一个独立的存在，不再只是个工具。Steinberger 让 CodexBar 更安全、更快，也更轻；Anthropic 给 Claude Code 加了 Auto Mode，还证明了 benchmark 分数会因为 eval infra 不同而大幅波动。Levie 警告说，AI agents 会逼着大家不停重写。

builderInsights.title: Guillermo Rauch
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: Design 正在变成 autonomous 的能力，而不是一个 tool
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他说，真正的变化不是 Figma 和 Claude Design 谁赢谁输，而是 design 正在变成 agent 可以直接调用的能力，不再是只靠人手工跑的 workflow。他已经看到像 v0、Flint 这类 agentic system，几乎不用 prompt，甚至完全不用 prompt，就能生成并持续维护 design、brand，甚至 site content。更大的押注是，这会走向 autonomous company：growth 和 advertising 也会交给 agent 来做。

tweets.text:
Whether design belongs in Figma or Claude Design is a distraction from a bigger shift.

1️⃣ Design will become autonomous. More helpful to think of it as 𝙳𝙴𝚂𝙸𝙶𝙽.𝚖𝚍, used by your coding agents running your software factory.

2️⃣ Specialized “personal” design tools generated by teams will proliferate. Design is a capability, not a tool. I agree with @rsms that there are many facets of design, and multiple tools are required.

I love prompting in @v0 and it’s become the place where I can channel my inspiration, explore, communicate. But I’m also seeing a new generation of products that use the v0 Platform API or Sandbox and put design on autopilot. 

There are next-generation agents like @tryflint and https://t.co/fZ7TO6rczt generating design & brand systems and maintaining them autonomously. Flint can even keep your website and content up to date and its design consistent. No human prompting needed. 

From this we will see the emergence of fully autonomous companies with agents like https://t.co/YhOmGYYwgE and https://t.co/v8TuIxujwy, which go a step further and grow and advertise your business.

tl:dr; The future looks very different from the present. AI is a true discontinuity. The “here’s the existing thing but with AI and ${jobTitle} is cooked” is short-sighted.

builderInsights.authorBio: partner @fpvventures - investing in seed/A. previous: early hire @meter, @opendoor, @atlassian & others. love @shimoleejhaveri + 👦👧

builderInsights.authorBio: ceo @box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
builderInsights.title: Aaron Levie
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: AI agent 会逼着你不断重写系统
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他说，做 agent 的团队要做好心理准备：每隔几个月就得把旧架构拆掉重来。因为模型在进步，昨天刚做完的东西很快就过时了。更大的变化是，软件不再只是 tech company 的事了。以后每个行业都需要 engineer 去把 agent 接起来，重做 workflow，并维护自动化带来的那些系统。

tweets.text:
It’s remarkable how often you need to be dramatically upgrading your AI architecture given the pace of progress in AI models right now. 

If you’re building agents, you basically need to throw away large parts of previous work that you setup to compensate for model limitations every few quarters. The systems you built to mitigate context window limits aren’t useful anymore, and for many use-cases it’s easier just to throw more compute at a problem today in ways that wouldn’t have worked previously.

If you’re deploying agents in a workflow, you likely need to equally be rethinking your core systems at about that same frequency. The way you would deploy agents in an enterprise 18 months ago is entirely different from the best practices that you’d have today.

This is partly why everyone’s working so hard right now. Right as a best practice is solidified, models improve dramatically, and that old work is rendered obsolete. Unclear that this lets up anytime soon, which is why the it pays to be so wired in right now.

When people think software engineers, they tend to think jobs at tech companies building apps. The growth of the tech industry is so wired into our heads that that’s what we think the point and limit of software is. That’s just not how to think about software anymore.

AI agents make it so every other company on the planet starts to create software for bringing automation to their workflows in a way that would have either been infeasible technically or unaffordable economically before.

Every biopharma, industrial company, consulting firm, bank, and retailer will be building far more software in the form of backend systems, data processing, new digital experiences for clients, automating end-to-end workflows, and more. Small businesses will light up projects that would have needed a team of 50 to go do, but now becomes plausible with 5 people. And companies will start hiring engineers to help them make agents work and help redesign their business processes.

All of this work will require people that are technical and skilled at software. The job becomes thinking through system design, wiring up various platforms, directing agents on what to automate, maintaining the system, upgrading it when things change, reviewing the output from the agents, and more.

Just for fun, I went to Eli Lilly’s career site to see what jobs were open, and lo and behold they have a category called “Lab Automation Software Engineer”. There will be jobs like these in every company going forward, and they are the jobs that represent a new category of work that is now becoming available. And it’s only one of many examples of what engineers will be doing in the future. 

So before you think it’s the end of engineering, imagine the demand in the real world for this kind of work.

podcastHighlights.source: The MAD Podcast with Matt Turck
podcastHighlights.title: Anthropic 的 Felix Rieseberg：Claude Cowork、Mythos，以及 SaaS 的消亡
podcastHighlights.suggestedTitle: Anthropic 押注未来属于本地、可信的 AI
podcastHighlights.suggestedSummary:
**核心结论：** Felix Rieseberg 认为，AI 真正的突破不在于模型本身有多强，而在于把这种能力变成可信、本地、好用的工作方式。

- Mythos 算是一次跃迁，因为它能找出 security flaw，还能以一种“既厉害又有点吓人”的方式把 software 搞崩。
- Cowork 的优势不是花哨 UI，而是 sandboxed computer、text-file skills 和 memory。它们让 model 真正能用，而且不用人盯着。
- 最大的 product gap 不是 model capability，而是 workflow design：既然“execution 基本是免费的”，真正的瓶颈就是 trust、context 和 taste。

Felix Rieseberg 现在在 Anthropic 负责 Claude Cowork 的 engineering。之前他在 Slack、Stripe 和 Notion 做过 product 和 engineering。他的观点很直接：AI 进化很快，但最后赢的产品，会是那些贴着用户现有工作方式来的产品——在 laptop 上，在文件里，在真实的 permission 和习惯里。这也是他为什么这么看好 local-first AI。他说：“Gmail 加上我的 login information 确实很有用。”这句话把抽象的 cloud access 和真实工作里的混乱现实，直接分开了。

他最反直觉的判断是：model 往往不是瓶颈。更难的是，怎么把 intelligence 包装成让人愿意信的东西。Cowork 用 virtual machine、connectors 和简单的 markdown “skills”，让 Claude 更像一个同事，而不是一个 chatbot。Felix 说，model 可以通过纯文本文件学会怎么订机票、遵守 style guide，或者记住偏好——不需要什么 fancy database。memory 也是 text。

重点就在这里。这种简单才是关键。Anthropic 的新 model Mythos 也许能找到 security hole，甚至在逃出 sandbox 后给 researcher 发邮件，但 Felix 真正关心的是更安全的 leverage：给人们更强的软件能力，同时不让他们交出 control。

blogUpdates.title: agentic coding evals 中的基础设施噪声量化
blogUpdates.suggestedTitle: Anthropic：benchmark 分数会随着 eval 基础设施变化
blogUpdates.suggestedSummary:
导语：Anthropic 发现，agentic coding benchmark 的结果只因为基础设施不同，就可能上下浮动几个点。Terminal-Bench 2.0 和 SWE-bench 都显示，资源设置会实打实影响分数。

数字：
- Terminal-Bench 2.0：从严格的 1x 限制切到不设上限的资源，整体 success 变化了 6 个百分点。
- 基础设施错误率从严格限制下的 5.8% 降到不设上限时的 0.5%。
- 从 1x 提到 3x headroom 后，基础设施错误降到 2.1%，分数变化也还在噪声范围内（p=0.40）。
- SWE-bench：分数随着 RAM 增加单调上升，在 227 个问题上，达到 5x baseline 时提升了 +1.54 分。

所以呢：核心结论很简单，agentic eval 不只是 model test，更像端到端 system test。container 限制、kill threshold、latency、cluster 行为，都会改变量到的东西。Anthropic 建议，每个 task 都要同时写清楚 guaranteed allocation 和 hard ceiling，然后把两者之间的 gap 调到合适。这样 floor 和 ceiling 下的分数才会都落在噪声范围内。原文说得很直白：leaderboard 上领先 2 分，可能真的是能力更强，也可能只是某个 eval 跑在更猛的硬件上。对 builder 和 benchmark 使用者来说，除非 eval 配置写清楚而且对齐了，否则小幅领先都要先打个问号。

blogUpdates.title: Claude Code auto mode：更安全地跳过 permissions
blogUpdates.suggestedTitle: Anthropic 为 Claude Code 加上 Auto Mode
blogUpdates.suggestedSummary:
**重点：**Anthropic 给 Claude Code 加了 **Auto mode**。它介于“每次都要点批准”和“完全跳过 permissions”之间，更稳一点。系统会用 model-based classifiers 判断哪些操作可以不经过人手点击就直接跑。

**数据：**
- 手动 prompt 的接受率是 **93%**，这也是 approval fatigue 的来源之一。
- 内部评估里，有 **10,000** 次真实 tool calls、**52** 次真实的过度激进行为，以及 **1,000** 次 synthetic exfiltration 尝试。
- 第 1 级 classifier：在真实流量上 **8.5% FPR**、**6.6% FNR**；在 synthetic exfil 上 **1.8% FNR**。
- 完整 pipeline：在真实过度激进行为上 **0.4% FPR**、**17% FNR**；在 synthetic exfil 上 **5.7% FNR**。

**所以呢：**Auto mode 面向的是这类用户：想少看点 permission prompt，但又不想把 guardrails 全关掉。它会拦住一些高风险动作，比如 scope escalation、credential hunting、data exfiltration 和绕过 safety checks；同时保留常规 repo edits 和安全 tools。Anthropic 说，这套系统的目标是抓住“**那些和 user intent 不一致的危险动作**”。他们也建议把它当成 `--dangerously-skip-permissions` 的更安全替代方案，而不是在高风险 infrastructure 上替代谨慎的人审。这个 feature 还加了 prompt-injection probe 和两阶段 classifier pipeline。用户也可以在 settings 里自定义 trusted environments 和 block rules。
